equivalence_argument: BuilderContext::new is still a simple constructor and does not create or spawn an executor registry service.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Builder startup does not create or spawn a background ExecutorRegistryService with the shared registry.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/node/builder/src/builder/mod.rs BuilderContext::new returns Self { head, provider, executor, config_container } only.
No audited repo contains ExecutorRegistryService::new or registry_service.start.

rationale: The primary commit subject explicitly says this integrates ExecutorRegistryService startup for early service initialization, and the diff wires ExecutorRegistryService::new plus executor.spawn(registry_service.start()) directly into BuilderContext::new before SpcVerifier construction. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Every BuilderContext::new call creates an ExecutorRegistryService from the node provider, environment-derived registry config, and the same shared ExecutorRegistry later passed to SpcVerifier, then spawns that service in the background before returning the builder context.
suggested_falsification_tests: Instantiate BuilderContext::new with a test TaskExecutor and mocked provider/config, instrument ExecutorRegistryService::start or its dependencies, and assert that a background service task is spawned with the same Arc<RwLock<ExecutorRegistry>> used to construct SpcVerifier.
summary: Builder startup does not launch an executor registry service.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct BuilderContext::new with an instrumented TaskExecutor and assert it spawns ExecutorRegistryService with the shared registry; current construction performs no spawn.
title: Builder startup launches executor registry service
